content: {"notes": [{"url": "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12738305/", "title": "Intermittent fasting improves metabolic outcomes in ... - PMC - NIH", "summary": "Abstract Background Previous studies have demonstrated that intermittent fasting (IF) has garnered scientific attention and gained recognition for its beneficial effects on metabolic outcomes. Accordingly, this systematic review and meta-analysis aimed to evaluate the effect of fasting on glycemic control, lipid profile, and inflammatory markers. Methods Databases such as PubMed, Embase, Cochrane, Scopus, and Web of Science were used to retrieve relevant studies published until September 2025.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Methods Databases such as PubMed, Embase, Cochrane, Scopus, and Web of Science were used to retrieve relevant studies published until September 2025.", "Moreover, the regimen successfully exerted its beneficial effect on low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (LDL-C) (SMD = −0.34; 95% CI: −0.53, −0.14; p = 0.001) and interleukin-6 (IL-6) (SMD = −0.30; 95% CI: −0.57, −0.03; p = 0.029) levels as well.", "No evidence of publication bias was detected using Egger’s and Begg’s test (p > 0.05).", "Conclusion The findings suggest that intermittent fasting may have favorable effects on the metabolic panel, specifically, FBS, insulin, HOMA-IR, (HbA1c), LDL-C, and IL-6 levels.", "In addition, the study protocol was approved by the International Prospective Register of Systematic Reviews (CRD420251142741). 2.1."], "knowledge_contribution": "Adds per-page evidence on the research topic: Methods Databases such as PubMed, Embase, Cochrane, Scopus, and Web of Science were used to retrieve relevant studies published until Septem"}, {"url": "https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0271531725000600", "title": "The metabolic effects of intermittent fasting in patients with type 2 ...", "summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT: page appears to be paywall/captcha/shell.", "perspectives": [], "key_facts": [], "knowledge_contribution": ""}, {"url": "https://www.thelancet.com/journals/eclinm/article/PIIS2589-5370(24)00098-1/fulltext", "title": "Intermittent fasting and health outcomes: an umbrella review of ...", "summary": "Harris, L. ∙ McGarty, A. ∙ Hutchison, L. ∙ et al. 19(1):"}, {"url": "https://touchendocrinology.com/diabetes/journal-articles/effect-of-intermittent-fasting-on-glycaemic-control-in-patients-with-type-2-diabetes-mellitus-a-systematic-review-and-meta-analysis-of-randomized-controlled-trials/", "title": "Effect of Intermittent Fasting on Glycaemic Control in Patients With ...", "summary": "Type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) is a severe public health issue notably impacting human life and health expenditure. Around 9.3% (463 million people) of the global population were living with diabetes in 2019, and this is projected to increase to 10.2% (578 million people) by 2030 and 10.9% (700 million people) by 2045.1,2 Diabetes impacts functional capacity and quality of life, and ultimately causes significant morbidity and premature mortality. Furthermore, the small sample sizes of these studies prevent drawing of firm conclusions.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Around 9.3% (463 million people) of the global population were living with diabetes in 2019, and this is projected to increase to 10.2% (578 million people) by 2030 and 10.9% (700 million people) by 2045.1,2 Diabetes impacts functional capacity and q", "We conducted a comprehensive search of electronic databases, including PubMed, Embase and Google Scholar, for articles published before 24 April 2022, regardless of area.", "The criteria for exclusion were: (1) trials without a control group, or other study designs; (2) studies that lack a HbA1c factor as an outcome or did not provide enough information; (3) non-human samples, reviews and case studies; (4) studies that w", "Cienfuegas et al. and Harvie et al. have performed studies with outcomes measured at different levels or time.", "Risk of bias assessment Using the updated Cochrane risk of bias assessment tool for randomized trials,26 two independent reviewers assessed the likelihood of bias in trials based on the outcomes (HbA1c or fasting glucose)."], "knowledge_contribution": "Adds per-page evidence on the research topic: Around 9.3% (463 million people) of the global population were living with diabetes in 2019, and this is projected to increase to 10.2% (578"}, {"url": "https://www.researchgate.net/publication/398583827_Intermittent_fasting_improves_metabolic_outcomes_in_metabolic_syndrome_a_systematic_review_and_meta-analysis_with_GRADE_evaluation", "title": "a systematic review and meta-analysis with GRADE evaluation", "summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT: page appears to be paywall/captcha/shell.", "perspectives": [], "key_facts": [], "knowledge_contribution": ""}, {"url": "https://www.cureus.com/articles/304835-the-role-of-intermittent-fasting-on-metabolic-syndrome-a-systematic-review-and-meta-analysis", "title": "The Role of Intermittent Fasting on Metabolic Syndrome", "summary": "Abstract Intermittent fasting has gained popularity as a dietary intervention to improve metabolic health. Metabolic syndrome may benefit from intermittent fasting by improving weight, cholesterol levels, blood pressure (BP), and glucose control. This study aims to assess the effects of intermittent fasting on weight, BMI, cholesterol levels, BP, and glucose in individuals with metabolic syndrome.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Analysis showed significant improvements in weight, with a mean reduction of 3.59 kg (95% CI: -4.59 to -2.59, p < 0.0001) and a decrease in BMI of 1.39 kg/m2 (95% CI: -1.87 to -0.92, p < 0.0001).", "Low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol levels dropped by 56.22 mg/dL (95% CI: -80.14 to -32.29, p < 0.0001), and systolic BP decreased by 5.54 mmHg (95% CI: -7.55 to -3.53, p < 0.0001).", "These risk factors include elevated blood pressure (BP), high blood sugar, excess body fat around the waist, and abnormal cholesterol or triglyceride levels [2].", "The fasting periods vary in length depending on the method, with some protocols involving fasting for 16 hours a day (such as the 16:8 method) or fasting for 24 hours once or twice a week (as seen in the 5:2 method) [6].", "This leads to a reduction in body fat and weight, which can help manage or even reverse conditions associated with metabolic syndrome [8]."], "knowledge_contribution": "Adds per-page evidence on the research topic: Analysis showed significant improvements in weight, with a mean reduction of 3.59 kg (95% CI: -4.59 to -2.59, p < 0.0001) and a decrease in "}]}
